steps.title: Identify the right triangle configuration
steps.content: The diagram shows triangle $QRS$ with a right angle at $R$ (marked by the small square). The bottom side $SR = 47$. Since the right angle is at $R$, the hypotenuse is $QS$ (the side opposite the right angle). The angle $Q$ at the top vertex is between sides $QR$ and $QS$.

steps.title: Apply the sine ratio
steps.content:
In a right triangle, $\sin(Q) = \frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{hypotenuse}}$. From angle $Q$'s perspective, the opposite side is $SR = 47$, and the hypotenuse is $QS$:

$$\sin(Q) = \frac{47}{QS}$$

Solving for $QS$:

$$QS = \frac{47}{\sin Q}$$

That matches choice D — D is correct.
